mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) over the prior three months signals strengthening consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or positive return frequency in top RSAFS quintile is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy tightens financial conditions and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS observed at 4.33 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and rises in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal labor market deterioration that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining PAYEMS and rising UNRATE produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side overheating that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in imminent tightening.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in late-cycle periods coincides with positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the conditional signal is below 0.2 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) erodes real corporate earnings and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL momentum fails to predict negative returns, with 60-day Rank IC turning positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1300 thousand units signals resilient construction demand and positive wealth effects that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST observed at 1321.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST exceeds 1300 thousand units, or the trigger underperforms the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and rising industrial production (INDPRO) confirms a non-inflationary growth environment that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling UNRATE and rising INDPRO coincides with negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term does not improve R-squared (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand strength that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to positively predict 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
